title: Shea T. Small
content:
Partner | Co-Head of M&A | Co-leader Global Metals & Mining
People.ContactEmail ssmall@mccarthy.ca
- British Columbia, 2013
- University of British Columbia
Shea Small, Co-Head of Mergers & Acquisitions and a member of the firm’s Board of Partners, has an international practice based in Toronto, Canada and London, UK, focusing on mergers & acquisitions, joint ventures, strategic investments, contested and special situations, private equity and capital markets.
Shea is Chambers -ranked, and repeatedly recognized by Lexpert, Best Lawyers , Legal 500 and IFLR 1000, for his significant experience in M&A, natural resources and international mandates. He also has an Acritas Star for his joint venture and global expertise.

#### Deeply Connected in the Metals and Mining Industry
Shea has significant industry expertise and deep industry connections in the global metals and mining sector, particularly in complex multi-jurisdictional transactions. With over 25 years of experience and as Co-Leader of McCarthy Tétrault’s Global Metals & Mining Group, Shea helps clients structure and implement transactions across the globe, with a particular focus on Australia, the UK, Latin America, West Africa and the Middle East.
Shea’s recent experience includes:
- Alpayana on its $250 million all cash hostile takeover of TSX-listed Sierra Metals which operates the Yauricocha mine in Peru and Bolivar mine in Mexico
- Canadian counsel to Rio Tinto on its US$6.7 billion all cash acquisition of NYSE/ASX-listed global lithium producer Arcadium Lithium
- Canadian counsel to global gold producer Newcrest Mining on its A$29 billion all stock acquisition by Newmont
- Rio Tinto on its US$3.3 billion all cash insider acquisition of TSX/NYSE-listed Turquoise Hill Resources which operated the Oyu Tolgoi copper and gold mine in Mongolia M&A Deal of the Year at the Canadian Law Awards
- M&A Deal of the Year at the Canadian Law Awards
- Canadian counsel to Ritchie Bros. on its US$7.3 billion cash and stock acquisition of NYSE-listed IAA, an online automobile auctioneer
- Newcrest Mining on its C$3.5 billion cash and stock acquisition of TSX/NYSE-listed Pretium Resources which operated the Brucejack gold mine in British Columbia
- Endeavour Mining on its C$2.44 billion all stock acquisition of TSX-listed Teranga Gold which operated the Sabodala-Massawa and Wahgnion gold mines in West Africa
- Endeavour Mining on its C$1 billion all stock acquisition of TSX/NASDAQ Stockholm-listed SEMAFO which operated the Boungou and Mana gold mines in Burkina Faso
- Canadian counsel to LSE-listed Acacia Mining on its US$428 million all stock acquisition by Barrick Gold
- Zijin Mining on its US$1 billion all cash acquisition of the Akyem gold mine in Ghana from Newmont
- Appian Capital on its acquisition of mineral sands deposits and processing facilities in the United States from Iluka Resources
- Egis on its acquisition of engineering consulting firm McIntosh Perry
- Zijin Mining on its US$360 million all cash acquisition of the Rosabel gold mine in Suriname from IAMGOLD
- Newcrest Mining on its US$460 million acquisition of gold prepay and silver stream facilities and offtake on Lundin Gold's Fruta del Norte gold mine in Ecuador from Orion and Blackstone
- Evolution Mining on its US$475 million cash and contingent value right acquisition from Newmont of the Red Lake gold mine in Ontario
- Stifel Financial on its acquisition of the Canadian capital markets business of GMP Capital
- Appian Capital on its acquisition of the Serrote da Laje copper project in Brazil from Aura Minerals
- Proterra Investment Partners on its majority acquisition of Tacora Resources which owns the Scully iron ore mine in Newfoundland and Labrador
- Rio Tinto on multiple sale processes for the Iron Ore Company of Canada
- OMERS on its acquisition of equity interests in Brit, a UK-based insurance company, and Eurolife, a Greek insurance company, from Fairfax
- Rio Tinto on its US$700 million acquisition of a 50% joint venture interest in the Matalco aluminium recycling business from Giampaolo Group
- Appian Capital on its US$100 million acquisition of a 60% joint venture interest in the Pine Point zinc project in the Northwest Territories from Osisko Metals
- Newcrest Mining on its US$800 million acquisition of a 70% joint venture interest in the Red Chris copper and gold mine in British Columbia from Imperial Metals
- Constellium on the establishment of an aluminum auto parts joint venture in Ontario

#### Strategic Investments
- Wyloo on its strategic investment to acquire 19.9% of the shares of TSX-listed Neo Performance Materials from Hastings Technology Metals
- Rio Tinto on its strategic investments to acquire equity interests in McEwen Copper which is developing the Los Azules copper project in Argentina
- La Mancha on the sale of its 35% equity investment in TSX/NYSE-listed Golden Star Resources and support for Chifeng Jilong Gold’s US$470 million cash acquisition of Golden Star Resources
- Appian Capital on the sale of its 13% equity investment in TSX-listed Roxgold and support for Fortuna Silver Mines’ C$1.1 billion stock and cash acquisition of Roxgold
- Wyloo on its strategic investment to acquire 23% of the shares and a convertible loan of TSXV-listed Noront Resources from Resource Capital Funds
- Newcrest Mining on its US$250 million strategic investment to acquire 27% of the shares of TSX-listed Lundin Gold which operates the Fruta del Norte gold mine in Ecuador
- La Mancha on its US$125.7 million strategic investment to acquire 30% of the shares of TSX and NYSE-listed Golden Star Resources which operated the Wassa and Prestea gold mines in Ghana

#### Contested and Special Situations
- Ramelius Resources on its potential acquisition of, and disputed merger contest with Westgold Resources for, TSX-listed Karora Resources which operated the Beta Hunt and Higginsville gold mines in Western Australia
- Wyloo on its C$615 million all cash insider acquisition of, and takeover battle with BHP over, TSXV-listed Noront Resources which owned several major nickel and chromite discoveries in Ontario’s Ring of Fire
- Viavi Solutions on its C$459 million cash offer for, and merger contest with the controlling founder of, TSX/NASDAQ-listed EXFO, a 5G equipment technology company
- Appian Capital on its US$1.2 billion agreed sale of the Atlantic nickel and Serrote da Laje copper mines in Brazil and subsequent termination by Sibanye-Stillwater
- Appian Capital on the sale to Silver Lake Resources of its debt and royalty interests as part of the CCAA insolvency process for TSX-listed Harte Gold
- Rio Tinto on its acquisition of the remaining 40% joint venture interest in the Diavik diamond mine in the Northwest Territories as part of the CCAA insolvency process for TSX/NYSE-listed Dominion Diamond Mines
- Detour Gold on its proxy battle with Paulson & Co.
- Oil Search on its US$2.2 billion stock and contingent value right offer for, and merger contest with Exxon Mobil over, InterOil, a NYSE-listed oil and gas company with project interests in Papua New Guinea
- Rio Tinto on its creep to control of, and shareholder rights plan arbitration with, TSX/NYSE-listed Turquoise Hill Resources (formerly Ivanhoe Mines)
- Allied Gold on its going public transaction, by way of reverse takeover, and US$267 million concurrent financing
- Ford , GE and Wells Fargo on their MTN, CP and maple debt programs in Canada
- RBC Capital Markets on RBC’s covered bond program in Canada
- dealer syndicates on debt offerings by First National Financial and Penske
- Rio Tinto on its standby commitments for multiple rights offerings by Turquoise Hill Resources
